conjecture_title: Informal remark on completeness of known forests satisfying Esperet's conjecture
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. II. Excluding a star-forest
summary: The remark in arXiv:2107.11780 that star-forests, double stars, K₂, and their induced subgraphs might constitute the complete list of forests H for which H-free graphs are polynomially χ-bounded is refuted: Nguyen (December 2025, arXiv:2512.24907) proved that P₅-free graphs are polynomially χ-bounded, and P₅ is a forest that is not an induced subgraph of any star, double star, or K₂. Separately, Briański–Davies–Walczak (2022, arXiv:2201.08814) disproved Esperet’s general conjecture by constructing hereditary χ-bounded classes that are not polynomially χ-bounded, confirming that polynomial χ-bounds are not automatic even within χ-bounded classes.

since_posted.title: Separating polynomial χ-boundedness from χ-boundedness
since_posted.authors: Marcin Briański, James Davies, Bartosz Walczak
since_posted.claim: Disproves Esperet’s general conjecture by constructing hereditary χ-bounded classes that are not polynomially χ-bounded, establishing that the question of which specific forests admit polynomial χ-bounds is non-trivial.

since_posted.title: Polynomial χ-boundedness for excluding P₅
since_posted.claim: Proves that P₅-free graphs are polynomially χ-bounded, exhibiting P₅ as a forest outside the class described in results 1.4–1.6 of arXiv:2107.11780 that satisfies the polynomial bound, directly disproving the completeness remark.

search_queries: Esperet conjecture polynomial chromatic number forests star-forest Scott Seymour Spirkl 2107.11780
forests polynomial chi-bounded Esperet conjecture double stars chromatic number induced subgraph
Brianki Davies Walczak Esperet conjecture counterexample chi-bounded polynomial 2022 2023
arxiv Brianki Davies Walczak separating polynomial chi-boundedness 2022 hereditary classes
notes: The authors themselves noted the completeness claim 'seems unlikely', and this is now confirmed: P₅ (a path on 5 vertices, not an induced subgraph of any star or double star) gives polynomial χ-bounds per Nguyen 2025. The general Esperet conjecture 1.2 was also independently disproved by Briański–Davies–Walczak (2201.08814). The class of forests admitting polynomial χ-bounds remains open but is strictly larger than what was known in 2021.
